result.markdown:
--- title: "2. MSstatsPTM — Phospho DE with protein-level adjustment" --- ## Overview Differential phospho-site abundance, adjusted for protein-level abundance changes from the bulk proteome pipeline. Uses MSstatsPTM, which fits paired models on PTM and protein datasets and reports both unadjusted (raw phospho) and adjusted (phospho minus protein) log2 fold changes per contrast. Same contrast set as the bulk pipeline: 4 pairwise + APP × Microglia interaction. ## Libraries ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(data.table) library(MSstats) library(MSstatsPTM) library(MSstatsConvert) library(qs2) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(stringr) library(tidyr) ``` ::: ## Directories and parameters ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} project_root <- "/nemo/lab/destrooperb/home/shared/zanettc/giulia_proteomics" phospho_base <- file.path(project_root, "phosphoproteomics") bulk_base <- file.path(project_root, "bulk_proteomics") run_num <- "run2" phospho_results_dir <- file.path(phospho_base, "results", run_num) phospho_objects_dir <- file.path(phospho_base, "data", "processed", run_num) phospho_input_dir <- file.path(phospho_base, "input") bulk_input_dir <- file.path(bulk_base, "input") dir.create(phospho_results_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE) dir.create(phospho_objects_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE) FASTA_PATH <- file.path(phospho_input_dir, "UP000000589_10090.fasta") PTM_LABEL <- "Phospho (STY)" # how Spectronaut writes the modification stopifnot("FASTA missing — see Setup notes" = file.exists(FASTA_PATH)) ``` ::: ## Build annotation (same 16-sample design) ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} msstats_annotation <- data.table( Run = paste0("GA_", 1:16), Condition = rep(c("hApp", "hAppNLGF", "hAppNLGF_FIRE", "hApp_FIRE"), each = 4), BioReplicate = 1:16 ) msstats_annotation ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
````` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} fwrite(msstats_annotation, file.path(phospho_objects_dir, "msstats_annotation.csv")) ``` ::: ## Load both Spectronaut reports (PTM and bulk) The bulk report serves as the "global protein" reference for adjustment. We read the same column subset that the bulk MSstats notebook uses. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} msstats_cols <- c( "R.Condition", "R.FileName", "R.Replicate", "PG.ProteinGroups", "PG.ProteinAccessions", "PEP.StrippedSequence", "EG.ModifiedSequence", "EG.PrecursorId", "EG.Qvalue", "FG.Charge", "FG.Id", "F.FrgIon", "F.Charge", "F.FrgLossType", "F.FrgNum", "F.FrgType", "F.NormalizedPeakArea", "F.PeakArea", "F.ExcludedFromQuantification" ) cat("Reading phospho report...\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Reading phospho report... ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} phospho_raw <- fread( file.path(phospho_input_dir, "PCF000612_phospho_GA_MSStats_report.csv"), select = msstats_cols ) cat("Phospho rows:", nrow(phospho_raw), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Phospho rows: 19232048 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("Reading bulk proteome report (for protein-level adjustment)...\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Reading bulk proteome report (for protein-level adjustment)... ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} protein_raw <- fread( file.path(bulk_input_dir, "20260423_154110_PCF000612_GA_MSStats_Report-Proteome.csv"), select = msstats_cols ) cat("Protein rows:", nrow(protein_raw), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Protein rows: 24057952 ``` ::: ::: ## Normalise phospho R.FileName + patch R.Condition / R.Replicate The phospho report writes sample IDs as `GA_P_1..GA_P_16` while the bulk proteome and the annotation use `GA_1..GA_16`. Strip the `GA_P_` prefix on the phospho data so both reports key against the same annotation. R.Condition is also "Not Defined" in both raw files — overwrite it from the annotation. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} phospho_raw[, R.FileName := stringr::str_replace(R.FileName, "^GA_P_", "GA_")] patch <- function(dt) { dt[msstats_annotation, on = c("R.FileName" = "Run"), `:=`(R.Condition = i.Condition, R.Replicate = i.BioReplicate)] dt } phospho_raw <- patch(phospho_raw) protein_raw <- patch(protein_raw) ``` ::: ## Convert to MSstatsPTM input format `SpectronauttoMSstatsPTMFormat()` returns a list of two data frames: `PTM` and `PROTEIN`. Both must be present for protein-level adjustment. The same `annotation` table is used for both — MSstatsPTM matches by `Run`. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ptm_input <- suppressWarnings(SpectronauttoMSstatsPTMFormat( input = phospho_raw, annotation = msstats_annotation, fasta_path = FASTA_PATH, protein_input = protein_raw, use_unmod_peptides = FALSE, # site-level: drop unmodified peptides mod_id = "\\[Phospho \\(STY\\)\\]", filter_with_Qvalue = TRUE, qvalue_cutoff = 0.01, removeProtein_with1Feature = FALSE )) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] "FASTA file missing 342 Proteins. These will be removed. This may be due to non-unique identifications." INFO [2026-04-29 22:44:16] ** Raw data from Spectronaut imported successfully. INFO [2026-04-29 22:44:17] ** Raw data from Spectronaut cleaned successfully. INFO [2026-04-29 22:44:17] ** Using provided annotation. INFO [2026-04-29 22:44:17] ** Run labels were standardized to remove symbols such as '.' or '%'. INFO [2026-04-29 22:44:17] ** The following options are used: - Features will be defined by the columns: PeptideSequence, PrecursorCharge, FragmentIon, ProductCharge - Shared peptides will be removed. - Proteins with single feature will not be removed. - Features with less than 3 measurements across runs will be removed. INFO [2026-04-29 22:44:20] ** Intensities with values of FExcludedFromQuantification equal to TRUE are replaced with NA WARN [2026-04-29 22:44:20] ** PGQvalue not found in input columns. INFO [2026-04-29 22:44:21] ** Intensities with values not smaller than 0.01 in EGQvalue are replaced with NA INFO [2026-04-29 22:44:23] ** Features with all missing measurements across runs are removed. INFO [2026-04-29 22:44:24] ** Shared peptides are removed. INFO [2026-04-29 22:45:24] ** Multiple measurements in a feature and a run are summarized by summaryforMultipleRows: max INFO [2026-04-29 22:45:25] ** Features with one or two measurements across runs are removed. INFO [2026-04-29 22:45:25] ** Run annotation merged with quantification data. INFO [2026-04-29 22:45:29] ** Features with one or two measurements across runs are removed. Missing values are marked by NA INFO [2026-04-29 22:45:38] ** Finished preprocessing. The dataset is ready to be processed by the dataProcess function. INFO [2026-04-29 22:45:39] ** Raw data from Spectronaut imported successfully. INFO [2026-04-29 22:45:44] ** Raw data from Spectronaut cleaned successfully. INFO [2026-04-29 22:45:44] ** Using annotation extracted from quantification data. INFO [2026-04-29 22:45:44] ** Run labels were standardized to remove symbols such as '.' or '%'. INFO [2026-04-29 22:45:44] ** The following options are used: - Features will be defined by the columns: PeptideSequence, PrecursorCharge, FragmentIon, ProductCharge - Shared peptides will be removed. - Proteins with single feature will not be removed. - Features with less than 3 measurements across runs will be removed. INFO [2026-04-29 22:45:49] ** Intensities with values of FExcludedFromQuantification equal to TRUE are replaced with NA WARN [2026-04-29 22:45:51] ** PGQvalue not found in input columns. INFO [2026-04-29 22:45:52] ** Intensities with values not smaller than 0.01 in EGQvalue are replaced with NA INFO [2026-04-29 22:45:56] ** Features with all missing measurements across runs are removed. INFO [2026-04-29 22:46:00] ** Shared peptides are removed. INFO [2026-04-29 22:46:58] ** Multiple measurements in a feature and a run are summarized by summaryforMultipleRows: max INFO [2026-04-29 22:47:01] ** Features with one or two measurements across runs are removed. INFO [2026-04-29 22:47:02] ** Run annotation merged with quantification data. INFO [2026-04-29 22:47:11] ** Features with one or two measurements across runs are removed. INFO [2026-04-29 22:47:12] ** Fractionation handled. INFO [2026-04-29 22:47:35] ** Updated quantification data to make balanced design. Missing values are marked by NA INFO [2026-04-29 22:47:36] ** Finished preprocessing. The dataset is ready to be processed by the dataProcess function. ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("PTM features: ", nrow(ptm_input$PTM), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` PTM features: 6206624 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("Protein features:", nrow(ptm_input$PROTEIN), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Protein features: 11946400 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} qs_save(ptm_input, file.path(phospho_objects_dir, "ptm_input.qs2")) ``` ::: ## Reload checkpoint (skip the read+convert on re-runs) ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ptm_input <- qs_read(file.path(phospho_objects_dir, "ptm_input.qs2")) ``` ::: ## Data summarisation (per dataset) `dataSummarizationPTM()` runs `dataProcess` on PTM and PROTEIN separately and returns both. TMP summarisation, median normalisation, MB-imputation — same defaults as the bulk pipeline. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} summarised <- suppressWarnings(dataSummarizationPTM( data = ptm_input, normalization = "equalizeMedians", summaryMethod = "TMP", censoredInt = "NA", MBimpute = TRUE, featureSubset = "highQuality", remove_uninformative_feature_outlier = TRUE )) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` INFO [2026-04-29 22:47:51] ** Features with one or two measurements across runs are removed. INFO [2026-04-29 22:47:51] ** Fractionation handled. INFO [2026-04-29 22:47:59] ** Updated quantification data to make balanced design. Missing values are marked by NA INFO [2026-04-29 22:48:11] ** Log2 intensities under cutoff = -2.7132 were considered as censored missing values. is done. ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} qs_save(summarised, file.path(phospho_objects_dir, "summarised_ptm.qs2")) ``` ::: ## Verify condition levels and build contrast matrix Same 4 pairwise + interaction as the bulk pipeline. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} summarised <- qs_read(file.path(phospho_objects_dir, "summarised_ptm.qs2")) condition_levels <- levels(summarised$PTM$ProteinLevelData$GROUP) cat("Condition levels (alphabetical):\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Condition levels (alphabetical): ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} print(condition_levels) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] "hApp" "hApp_FIRE" "hAppNLGF" "hAppNLGF_FIRE" ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} make_contrast <- function(numerator, denominator, levels) { v <- setNames(rep(0L, length(levels)), levels) v[numerator] <- 1L v[denominator] <- -1L v } interaction_weights <- c( hApp = -1, hAppNLGF = 1, hApp_FIRE = 1, hAppNLGF_FIRE = -1 ) make_linear_contrast <- function(weights, levels) { v <- setNames(rep(0, length(levels)), levels) v[names(weights)] <- weights stopifnot(abs(sum(v)) < 1e-9) v } comparison_matrix <- rbind( make_contrast("hAppNLGF", "hApp", condition_levels), make_contrast("hApp_FIRE", "hApp", condition_levels), make_contrast("hAppNLGF_FIRE", "hAppNLGF", condition_levels), make_contrast("hAppNLGF_FIRE", "hApp_FIRE", condition_levels), make_linear_contrast(interaction_weights, condition_levels) ) rownames(comparison_matrix) <- c( "hAppNLGF_vs_hApp", "hApp_FIRE_vs_hApp", "hAppNLGF_FIRE_vs_hAppNLGF", "hAppNLGF_FIRE_vs_hApp_FIRE", "Interaction_APP_x_Microglia" ) print(comparison_matrix) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` hApp hApp_FIRE hAppNLGF hAppNLGF_FIRE hAppNLGF_vs_hApp -1 0 1 0 hApp_FIRE_vs_hApp -1 1 0 0 hAppNLGF_FIRE_vs_hAppNLGF 0 0 -1 1 hAppNLGF_FIRE_vs_hApp_FIRE 0 -1 0 1 Interaction_APP_x_Microglia -1 1 1 -1 ``` ::: ::: ## Group comparison (adjusted for protein abundance) `groupComparisonPTM()` returns three result tables: - `PTM.Model` — raw PTM contrast results (unadjusted phospho changes) - `PROTEIN.Model` — protein-level contrast results (from the bulk reference) - `ADJUSTED.Model` — phospho minus protein per contrast → "phospho-specific" changes The adjusted table is what most phospho papers cite — it isolates regulation that isn't simply explained by total protein abundance. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ptm_results <- suppressWarnings(groupComparisonPTM( data = summarised, contrast.matrix = comparison_matrix, data.type = "LabelFree" )) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` INFO [2026-04-30 03:46:43] == Start to test and get inference in whole plot ... | | | 0% | | | 1% | |= | 1% | |= | 2% | |== | 2% | |== | 3% | |== | 4% | |=== | 4% | |=== | 5% write_table <- function(df, filename) { fwrite(as.data.table(df), file.path(phospho_results_dir, filename)) cat("Wrote", filename, ":", nrow(df), "rows\n") } write_table(ptm_results$PTM.Model, "PTM_unadjusted_GroupComparison.csv") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Wrote PTM_unadjusted_GroupComparison.csv : 218725 rows ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} write_table(ptm_results$PROTEIN.Model, "PROTEIN_GroupComparison.csv") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Wrote PROTEIN_GroupComparison.csv : 44590 rows ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} write_table(ptm_results$ADJUSTED.Model, "PTM_adjusted_GroupComparison.csv") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Wrote PTM_adjusted_GroupComparison.csv : 218725 rows ``` ::: ::: ## Quick summary of adjusted results ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} adj <- as.data.table(ptm_results$ADJUSTED.Model) adj[!is.na(adj.pvalue), .(n_tested = .N, n_sig = sum(adj.pvalue < 0.05), n_up = sum(adj.pvalue < 0.05 & log2FC > 0), n_down = sum(adj.pvalue < 0.05 & log2FC < 0)), by = Label][order(Label)] ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
````` ::: ::: ## Reload checkpoint ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ptm_results <- qs_read(file.path(phospho_objects_dir, "ptm_results.qs2")) ``` :::
